responseHeaders.vary: Accept-Encoding, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language, Origin, Access-Control-Request-Headers, Access-Control-Request-Method
responseHeaders.permissions-policy: xr-spatial-tracking=(self), geolocation=(self), midi=(), sync-xhr=(), microphone=(self), camera=(self), magnetometer=(), gyroscope=(), fullscreen=(self), payment=()
responseHeaders.content-security-policy: default-src * 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; script-src * data: https://* 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; connect-src * 'unsafe-inline'; img-src * data: blob: 'unsafe-inline'; frame-src * data: blob: ;frame-ancestors 'self'; style-src * data: https://* 'unsafe-inline';

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: These checks highlight opportunities to [improve the accessibility of your web app](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/accessibility/). Automatic detection can only detect a subset of issues and does not guarantee the accessibility of your web app, so [manual testing](https://web.dev/articles/how-to-review) is also encouraged.
accessibility.manualDescription: These items address areas which an automated testing tool cannot cover. Learn more in our guide on [conducting an accessibility review](https://web.dev/articles/how-to-review).

seo.title: SEO
seo.description: These checks ensure that your page is following basic search engine optimization advice. There are many additional factors Lighthouse does not score here that may affect your search ranking, including performance on [Core Web Vitals](https://web.dev/explore/vitals). [Learn more about Google Search Essentials](https://support.google.com/webmasters/answer/35769).
seo.manualDescription: Run these additional validators on your site to check additional SEO best practices.

technologies.name: Sentry
technologies.description: Sentry is an open-source platform for workflow productivity, aggregating errors from across the stack in real time.

technologies.name: Ruby
technologies.description: Ruby is an open-source object-oriented programming language.

technologies.name: Node.js
technologies.description: Node.js is an open-source, cross-platform, JavaScript runtime environment that executes JavaScript code outside a web browser.

technologies.name: Quill
technologies.description: Quill is a free open-source WYSIWYG editor.

technologies.name: Google Maps
technologies.description: Google Maps is a web mapping service. It offers satellite imagery, aerial photography, street maps, 360° interactive panoramic views of streets, real-time traffic conditions, and route planning for traveling by foot, car, bicycle and air, or public transportation.

technologies.name: Leaflet
technologies.description: Leaflet is the open-source JavaScript library for mobile-friendly interactive maps.

technologies.name: Ruby on Rails
technologies.description: Ruby on Rails is a server-side web application framework written in Ruby under the MIT License.

technologies.name: Bootstrap
technologies.description: Bootstrap is a free and open-source CSS framework directed at responsive, mobile-first front-end web development. It contains CSS and JavaScript-based design templates for typography, forms, buttons, navigation, and other interface components.

technologies.name: Marko
technologies.description: Marko is HTML re-imagined as a language for building dynamic and reactive user interfaces.

technologies.name: Varnish
technologies.description: Varnish is a reverse caching proxy.

technologies.name: React
technologies.description: React is an open-source JavaScript library for building user interfaces or UI components.

technologies.name: styled-components
technologies.description: Styled components is a CSS-in-JS styling framework that uses tagged template literals in JavaScript.

technologies.name: Cloudflare Browser Insights
technologies.description: Cloudflare Browser Insights is a tool that measures the performance of websites from the perspective of users.

technologies.name: lit-html
technologies.description: lit-html is a simple, modern, safe, small and fast HTML templating library for JavaScript.

technologies.name: lit-element
technologies.description: lit-element is a simple base class for creating web components that work in any web page with any framework. lit-element uses lit-html to render into shadow DOM, and adds API to manage properties and attributes.

technologies.name: Livefyre
technologies.description: Livefyre is a platform that integrates with the social web to boost social interaction.

technologies.name: jQuery
technologies.description: jQuery is a JavaScript library which is a free, open-source software designed to simplify HTML DOM tree traversal and manipulation, as well as event handling, CSS animation, and Ajax.

technologies.name: FancyBox
technologies.description: FancyBox is a tool for displaying images, html content and multi-media in a Mac-style 'lightbox' that floats overtop of web page.

technologies.name: core-js
technologies.description: core-js is a modular standard library for JavaScript, with polyfills for cutting-edge ECMAScript features.

technologies.name: Cloudflare Rocket Loader
technologies.description: Cloudflare Rocket Loader is responsible for prioritising over website's content by delaying the loading of Javascript until rendering.

technologies.name: Browser-Update.org
technologies.description: Browser-update.org is a tool to unobtrusively notify visitors that they should update their web browser in order to use your website.

technologies.name: reCAPTCHA
technologies.description: reCAPTCHA is a free service from Google that helps protect websites from spam and abuse.

technologies.name: HSTS
technologies.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) informs browsers that the site should only be accessed using HTTPS.

technologies.name: Cloudflare
technologies.description: Cloudflare is a web-infrastructure and website-security company, providing content-delivery-network services, DDoS mitigation, Internet security, and distributed domain-name-server services.

technologies.name: Webpack
technologies.description: Webpack is an open-source JavaScript module bundler.

technologies.name: Babel
technologies.description: Babel is a free and open-source transcompiler for writing next generation JavaScript.

technologies.name: PWA
technologies.description: Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) are web apps built and enhanced with modern APIs to deliver enhanced capabilities, reliability, and installability while reaching anyone, anywhere, on any device, all with a single codebase.

technologies.name: Open Graph
technologies.description: Open Graph is a protocol that is used to integrate any web page into the social graph.

technologies.name: Module Federation
technologies.description: Module Federation is a webpack technology for dynamically loading parts of other independently deployed builds.

technologies.name: HTTP/3
technologies.description: HTTP/3 is the third major version of the Hypertext Transfer Protocol used to exchange information on the World Wide Web.
